description: Curated quotes for Pricing Studio loading screens. Austrian economists, Bitcoin advocates, cypherpunks, sound money thinkers.

quotes.text: The task of economics is to foretell the remoter effects, and so to allow us to avoid such acts as attempt to remedy a present ill by sowing the seeds of a much greater ill for the future.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The curious task of economics is to demonstrate to men how little they really know about what they imagine they can design.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: There is no means of avoiding the final collapse of a boom brought about by credit expansion.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: If we wish to preserve a free society, it is essential that we recognize that the desirability of a particular object is not sufficient justification for the use of coercion.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Sound money is an instrument of protection of civil liberties against despotic inroads on the part of governments.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Every individual acts on the basis of the same principle on which the market economy is built — the urge to satisfy desires and to remove uneasiness.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Value does not exist outside the consciousness of man.
quotes.author: Carl Menger

quotes.text: Every step which leads from capitalism toward planning is necessarily a step nearer to absolutism and dictatorship.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Prices are an instrument of communication and guidance which embody more information than we directly have.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The root problem with conventional currency is all the trust that's required to make it work.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: I think the internet is going to be one of the major forces for reducing the role of government. The one thing that's missing is a reliable e-cash.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Bitcoin will do to banks what email did to the postal industry.
quotes.author: Rick Falkvinge

quotes.text: We have elected to put our money and faith in a mathematical framework that is free of politics and human error.
quotes.author: Tyler Winklevoss

quotes.text: The swarm is headed towards us.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: I do think Bitcoin is the first encrypted money that has the potential to do something like change the world.
quotes.author: Peter Thiel

quotes.text: Lost coins only make everyone else's coins worth slightly more. Think of it as a donation to everyone.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Give me control of a nation's money and I care not who makes its laws.
quotes.author: Mayer Amschel Rothschild

quotes.text: Money is the barometer of a society's virtue.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Inflation is taxation without legislation.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The government solution to a problem is usually as bad as the problem.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Nobody spends somebody else's money as carefully as he spends his own.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: A claim for equality of material position can be met only by a government with totalitarian powers.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: It is well enough that people of the nation do not understand our banking and monetary system, for if they did, I believe there would be a revolution before tomorrow morning.
quotes.author: Henry Ford

quotes.text: Gold is money. Everything else is credit.
quotes.author: J.P. Morgan

quotes.text: The art of economics consists in looking not merely at the immediate but at the longer effects of any act or policy.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: Economics is haunted by more fallacies than any other study known to man.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: The single most important thing about money is that it enables trade to take place without the parties to the trade trusting each other.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Government is the great fiction through which everybody endeavors to live at the expense of everybody else.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: When plunder becomes a way of life for a group of men in a society, over the course of time they create for themselves a legal system that authorizes it and a moral code that glorifies it.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: If the American people ever allow private banks to control the issue of their currency, the banks and corporations that will grow up around them will deprive the people of all property.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: Bitcoin is a remarkable cryptographic achievement and the ability to create something that is not duplicable in the digital world has enormous value.
quotes.author: Eric Schmidt

quotes.text: The Times 03/Jan/2009 Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Capitalism is what people do if you leave them alone.
quotes.author: Kenneth Minogue

quotes.text: The state is the great fictitious entity by which everyone seeks to live at the expense of everyone else.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: A society that puts equality before freedom will get neither. A society that puts freedom before equality will get a high degree of both.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Underlying most arguments against the free market is a lack of belief in freedom itself.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: I don't believe we shall ever have a good money again before we take the thing out of the hands of government.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Nothing is so permanent as a temporary government program.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Those who would give up essential Liberty, to purchase a little temporary Safety, deserve neither Liberty nor Safety.
quotes.author: Benjamin Franklin

quotes.text: In the absence of the gold standard, there is no way to protect savings from confiscation through inflation.
quotes.author: Alan Greenspan

quotes.text: Human action is purposeful behavior.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The entrepreneur profits only to the extent he has succeeded in serving the consumers better than other people have done.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: If you put the federal government in charge of the Sahara Desert, in five years there'd be a shortage of sand.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The first panacea for a mismanaged nation is inflation of the currency; the second is war. Both bring a temporary prosperity; both bring a permanent ruin.
quotes.author: Ernest Hemingway

quotes.text: Freedom and liberty always refer to interhuman relations. A man is free as far as he can live and get on without being at the mercy of arbitrary decisions on the part of other people.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Liberty is always freedom from the government.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Freedom really means the freedom to make mistakes.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Socialism is an alternative to capitalism as potassium cyanide is an alternative to water.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: A society that chooses between capitalism and socialism does not choose between two social systems; it chooses between social cooperation and the disintegration of society.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: All rational action is in the first place individual action. Only the individual thinks. Only the individual reasons. Only the individual acts.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Economics must not be relegated to classrooms and statistical offices and must not be left to esoteric circles. It is the philosophy of human life and action and concerns everybody and everything.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Value is not intrinsic; it is not in things. It is within us; it is the way in which man reacts to the conditions of his environment.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The worst evils which mankind has ever had to endure were inflicted by bad governments.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The market is not a place, a thing, or a collective entity. The market is a process.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Whoever wants peace among nations must seek to limit the state and its influence most strictly.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: If history could teach us anything, it would be that private property is inextricably linked with civilization.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The body of economic knowledge is an essential element in the structure of human civilization; it is the foundation upon which modern industrialism and all the moral, intellectual, technological, and therapeutical achievements of the last centuries have been built.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Capitalism needs neither propaganda nor apostles. Its achievements speak for themselves.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Modern civilization is a product of the philosophy of laissez faire.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Government cannot make man richer, but it can make him poorer.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Many who are self-taught far excel the doctors, masters, and bachelors of the most renowned universities.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The issue is always the same: the government or the market. There is no third solution.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Used to the conditions of a capitalistic environment, the average American takes it for granted that every year business improves and that every other year his standard of living rises.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: People do not cooperate under the division of labor because they love or should love one another. They cooperate because this best serves their own interests.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The gold standard did not collapse. Governments abolished it in order to pave the way for inflation.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Credit expansion is the governments' foremost tool in their struggle against the market economy.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Nothing is more securely lodged than the ignorance of the experts.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Liberty not only means that the individual has both the opportunity and the burden of choice; it also means that he must bear the consequences of his actions. Liberty and responsibility are inseparable.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: What our generation has forgotten is that the system of private property is the most important guarantee of freedom, not only for those who own property, but scarcely less for those who do not.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Emergencies have always been the pretext on which the safeguards of individual liberty have been eroded.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The more the state plans the more difficult planning becomes for the individual.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The advantage of a free market is that it allows millions of decision-makers to respond individually to freely determined prices, allocating resources in a manner that can't be mimicked by a central plan.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: We shall not grow wiser before we learn that much that we have done was very foolish.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: To act on the belief that we possess the knowledge and the power which enable us to shape the processes of society entirely to our liking, knowledge which in fact we do not possess, is likely to make us do much harm.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Even the striving for equality by means of a directed economy can result only in an officially enforced inequality — an authoritarian determination of the status of each individual in the new hierarchical order.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The argument for liberty is not an argument against organization, which is one of the most powerful tools human reason can employ, but against all exclusive, privileged, monopolistic organization.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: From the fact that people are very different it follows that, if we treat them equally, the result must be inequality in their actual position.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Perhaps the fact that we have seen millions voting themselves into complete dependence on a tyrant has made our generation understand that to choose one's government is not necessarily to secure freedom.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: If socialists understood economics, they wouldn't be socialists.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: It is no crime to be ignorant of economics, which is, after all, a specialized discipline. But it is totally irresponsible to have a loud and vociferous opinion on economic subjects while remaining in this state of ignorance.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: Taxation is nothing but organized theft, and the concept of a fair tax is therefore every bit as absurd as that of fair theft.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: The State is, and always has been, the great single enemy of the human race, its liberty, happiness, and progress.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: If you wish to know how libertarians regard the State and any of its acts, simply think of the State as a criminal band, and all of the libertarian attitudes will logically fall into place.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: It is in war that the State really comes into its own: swelling in power, in number, in pride, in absolute dominion over the economy and the society.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: Capitalism is the fullest expression of anarchism, and anarchism is the fullest expression of capitalism.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: The State is in no sense required by the nature of man; quite the contrary.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: What the government is good at is collecting taxes, taking away your freedoms, and killing people. It's not good at much else.
quotes.author: Tom Clancy

quotes.text: The State acquires all its funds by coercion, i.e. taxation. And taxation is simply robbery: the taking of money by force from the producer.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: Since the State necessarily lives by the compulsory confiscation of private capital, and since its expansion necessarily involves ever-greater incursions on private individuals and private enterprise, we must assert that the state is profoundly and inherently anti-capitalist.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: The man who produces while others dispose of his product is a slave.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: There is only one difference between a bad economist and a good one: the bad economist confines himself to the visible effect; the good economist takes into account both the effect that can be seen and those effects that must be foreseen.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: If the natural tendencies of mankind are so bad that it is not safe to permit people to be free, how is it that the tendencies of these organizers are always good?
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: Life, liberty, and property do not exist because men have made laws. On the contrary, it was the fact that life, liberty, and property existed beforehand that caused men to make laws in the first place.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: Everyone wants to live at the expense of the state. They forget that the state wants to live at the expense of everyone.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: Sometimes the law defends plunder and participates in it. Sometimes the law places the whole apparatus of judges, police, prisons and gendarmes at the service of the plunderers, and treats the victim — when he defends himself — as a criminal.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: The law perverted! The law — and, in its wake, all the collective forces of the nation — the law, I say, not only turned from its proper purpose but made to follow an entirely contrary purpose!
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: Legal plunder can be committed in an infinite number of ways. Thus we have an infinite number of plans for organizing it.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: The bad economist sees only what immediately strikes the eye; the good economist also looks beyond.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: The world is full of so-called economists who in turn are full of schemes for getting something for nothing.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: The broken-window fallacy, under a hundred disguises, is the most persistent in the history of economics.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: Everything we get, outside of the free gifts of nature, must in some way be paid for.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: Mere inflation — that is, the mere issuance of more money, with the consequence of higher wages and prices — may look like the creation of more demand. But in terms of the actual production and exchange of real things it is not.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: When people have learned to regard money as something sacred, they are no longer willing to consider it as a means to an end, but begin to regard it as an end in itself.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: The first lesson of economics is scarcity: There is never enough of anything to satisfy all those who want it. The first lesson of politics is to disregard the first lesson of economics.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: It is hard to imagine a more stupid or more dangerous way of making decisions than by putting those decisions in the hands of people who pay no price for being wrong.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: It is amazing that people who think we cannot afford to pay for doctors, hospitals, and medication somehow think that we can afford to pay for doctors, hospitals, medication and a government bureaucracy to administer it.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: The real minimum wage is always zero, regardless of the laws, and that is the wage that many workers receive in the wake of the creation or escalation of a government-mandated minimum wage.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: Competition does a much more effective job than government at protecting consumers.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: Since this is an era when many people are concerned about fairness and social justice, what is your fair share of what someone else has worked for?
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: One of the common failings among honorable people is a failure to appreciate how thoroughly dishonorable some other people can be, and how dangerous it is to trust them.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: Socialism in general has a record of failure so blatant that only an intellectual could ignore or evade it.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: Much of the social history of the Western world, over the past three decades, has been a history of replacing what worked with what sounded good.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: People who enjoy meetings should not be in charge of anything.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: The most basic question is not what is best, but who shall decide what is best.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: Each new generation born is in effect an invasion of civilization by little barbarians, who must be civilized before it is too late.
quotes.author: Thomas Sowell

quotes.text: I've been working on a new electronic cash system that's fully peer-to-peer, with no trusted third party.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: If you don't believe it or don't get it, I don't have the time to try to convince you, sorry.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: It might make sense just to get some in case it catches on. If enough people think the same way, that becomes a self-fulfilling prophecy.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: The nature of Bitcoin is such that once version 0.1 was released, the core design was set in stone for the rest of its lifetime.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Writing a description for this thing for general audiences is bloody hard. There's nothing to relate it to.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: I'm sure that in 20 years there will either be very large transaction volume or no volume.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: The utility of the exchanges made possible by Bitcoin will far exceed the cost of electricity used. Therefore, not having Bitcoin would be the net waste.
quotes.author: Satoshi Nakamoto

quotes.text: Bitcoin is the first practical solution to the problem of digital scarcity.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: Trustworthy third parties are security holes.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: I was trying to mimic as closely as possible in cyberspace the security and trust characteristics of gold, and chief among those is that it doesn't depend on a trusted central authority.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: It would be very nice if there were a protocol whereby unforgeably costly bits could be created online with minimal dependence on trusted third parties.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: The problem, in a nutshell, is that our money currently depends on trust in a third party for its value.
quotes.author: Nick Szabo

quotes.text: Privacy is necessary for an open society in the electronic age.
quotes.author: Eric Hughes

quotes.text: Cypherpunks write code. We know that someone has to write software to defend privacy, and we're going to write it.
quotes.author: Eric Hughes

quotes.text: Cypherpunks deplore regulations on cryptography, for encryption is fundamentally a private act.
quotes.author: Eric Hughes

quotes.text: We must defend our own privacy if we expect to have any. We must come together and create systems which allow anonymous transactions to take place.
quotes.author: Eric Hughes

quotes.text: Running bitcoin.
quotes.author: Hal Finney

quotes.text: There is no second best.
quotes.author: Michael Saylor

quotes.text: Bitcoin is the most efficient system in the history of mankind for channeling energy through time and space.
quotes.author: Michael Saylor

quotes.text: Bitcoin is the first software network capable of storing all the monetary energy in the world with no loss of power over time and negligible transmission loss.
quotes.author: Michael Saylor

quotes.text: The Bitcoin price is set by those with more money and less knowledge than you. In time, they will get the knowledge and you will get the money.
quotes.author: Michael Saylor

quotes.text: Bitcoin is hope.
quotes.author: Michael Saylor

quotes.text: Bitcoin is a bank in cyberspace, run by incorruptible software, offering a global, affordable, simple, and secure savings account to billions of people that don't have the option or desire to run their own hedge fund.
quotes.author: Andreas Antonopoulos

quotes.text: Bitcoin is not just money for the internet. It's the internet of money.
quotes.author: Andreas Antonopoulos

quotes.text: Money is the killer app.
quotes.author: Andreas Antonopoulos

quotes.text: Capitalism was the only system in history where wealth was not acquired by looting, but by production, not by force, but by trade.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Money demands that you sell, not your weakness to men's stupidity, but your talent to their reason.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Wealth is the product of man's capacity to think.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: The question isn't who is going to let me; it's who is going to stop me.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Run for your life from any man who tells you that money is evil. That sentence is the leper's bell of an approaching looter.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Money is only a tool. It will take you wherever you wish, but it will not replace you as the driver.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: So you think that money is the root of all evil? Have you ever asked what is the root of money?
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: The smallest minority on earth is the individual. Those who deny individual rights cannot claim to be defenders of minorities.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Individual rights are not subject to a public vote; a majority has no right to vote away the rights of a minority.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Decentralization is based on the simple notion that it is easier to macrobullshit than microbullshit.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: If we do not decentralize and distribute responsibility, it will happen by itself, the hard way.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: Wind extinguishes a candle and energizes fire. You want to be the fire and wish for the wind.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: Antifragility is beyond resilience or robustness. The resilient resists shocks and stays the same; the antifragile gets better.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: The three most harmful addictions are heroin, carbohydrates, and a monthly salary.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: If you see fraud and do not say fraud, you are a fraud.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: Bureaucracy is a construction by which a person is conveniently separated from the consequences of his or her actions.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: Don't tell me what you think, tell me what you have in your portfolio.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: A system that doesn't have a mechanism of skin in the game will eventually blow up.
quotes.author: Nassim Nicholas Taleb

quotes.text: The Fed is the prime source of our economic problems. Our nation needs sound money again.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: A system of capitalism presumes sound money, not fiat money manipulated by a central bank.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: Everyone who believes in freedom must work diligently for sound money, fully redeemable. Nothing else is compatible with the humanitarian goals of peace and prosperity.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: The moral and constitutional obligations of our representatives in Washington are to protect our liberty, not coddle the world, precipitating no-win wars, while bringing bankruptcy and economic turmoil to our people.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: What we need is not more government spending but rather less government interference in the economy.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: The most important element of a free society, where individual rights are held in the highest esteem, is the rejection of the initiation of violence.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: When one gets in bed with government, one must expect the diseases it spreads.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: Gold has served as the principal medium of exchange throughout history because its value does not depend on a government fulfilling its promises.
quotes.author: Ron Paul

quotes.text: Democracy is a soft variant of communism, and rarely in the history of ideas has it been taken for anything else.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: Egalitarianism, in every form and shape, is incompatible with the idea of private property.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: Private property implies exclusivity, inequality, and difference.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: A member of the human race who is completely incapable of understanding the higher productivity of labor performed under a division of labor based on private property is not properly speaking a person but falls instead in the same moral category as an animal.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: No one is allowed to own the means of production privately, and so there is no buying or selling of productive assets. Since money is nothing but a medium of exchange, with no money and no exchange, there is also no need for money.
quotes.author: Hans-Hermann Hoppe

quotes.text: Interest is not an accidental category which makes its appearance only in our individualistic and capitalist society; but an economic category which springs from elementary economic causes.
quotes.author: Eugen von Böhm-Bawerk

quotes.text: Present goods are, as a rule, worth more than future goods of like kind and number.
quotes.author: Eugen von Böhm-Bawerk

quotes.text: Value is nothing inherent in goods, no property of them. It is a judgment economizing men make about the importance of goods at their disposal for the maintenance of their lives and well-being.
quotes.author: Carl Menger

quotes.text: Man himself is the beginning and the end of every economy.
quotes.author: Carl Menger

quotes.text: The quantities of consumption goods at human disposal are limited, while human wants are, if not infinite, at least indefinite in number and degree.
quotes.author: Carl Menger

quotes.text: An economy requires economizing; it requires stewardship.
quotes.author: Carl Menger

quotes.text: Property rights are not the rights of property. They are the rights of humans with regard to property.
quotes.author: James Bovard

quotes.text: Democracy must be something more than two wolves and a sheep voting on what to have for dinner.
quotes.author: James Bovard

quotes.text: The Federal Government is the great protector, and the government's own abuses are the greatest danger.
quotes.author: James Bovard

quotes.text: Concentrated power is not rendered harmless by the good intentions of those who create it.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: One of the great mistakes is to judge policies and programs by their intentions rather than their results.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Only government can take perfectly good paper, cover it with perfectly good ink, and make the combination worthless.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The most important single central fact about a free market is that no exchange takes place unless both parties benefit.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: There is one and only one social responsibility of business — to use its resources and engage in activities designed to increase its profits so long as it stays within the rules of the game.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: A major source of objection to a free economy is precisely that it gives people what they want instead of what a particular group thinks they ought to want.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The great virtue of a free market system is that it does not care what color people are; it does not care what their religion is; it only cares whether they can produce something you want to buy.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Is it really true that political self-interest is nobler somehow than economic self-interest?
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Most economic fallacies derive from the tendency to assume that there is a fixed pie, that one party can gain only at the expense of another.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: Hell hath no fury like a bureaucrat scorned.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The great advances of civilization, whether in architecture or painting, in science or in literature, in industry or agriculture, have never come from centralized government.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: We are spending 51 percent of the national income through the government. That's a frightfully large amount.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: History suggests that capitalism is a necessary condition for political freedom. Clearly it is not a sufficient condition.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: The way to crush the bourgeoisie is between the millstones of taxation and inflation.
quotes.author: Vladimir Lenin

quotes.text: Paper money eventually returns to its intrinsic value: zero.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: By a continuing process of inflation, governments can confiscate, secretly and unobserved, an important part of the wealth of their citizens.
quotes.author: John Maynard Keynes

quotes.text: There is no subtler, no surer means of overturning the existing basis of society than to debauch the currency.
quotes.author: John Maynard Keynes

quotes.text: In the long run we are all dead.
quotes.author: John Maynard Keynes

quotes.text: Necessity is the plea for every infringement of human freedom. It is the argument of tyrants; it is the creed of slaves.
quotes.author: William Pitt the Younger

quotes.text: The natural effort of every individual to better his own condition is so powerful that it is alone, and without any assistance, capable of carrying on the society to wealth and prosperity.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: It is not from the benevolence of the butcher, the brewer, or the baker, that we expect our dinner, but from their regard to their own interest.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: Every individual necessarily labours to render the annual revenue of the society as great as he can. He is led by an invisible hand to promote an end which was no part of his intention.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: To prohibit a great people from making all that they can of every part of their own produce, or from employing their stock and industry in the way that they judge most advantageous to themselves, is a manifest violation of the most sacred rights of mankind.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: There is no art which one government sooner learns of another than that of draining money from the pockets of the people.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: The desire of food is limited in every man by the narrow capacity of the human stomach; but the desire of the conveniences and ornaments of building, dress, equipage, and household furniture seems to have no limit or certain boundary.
quotes.author: Adam Smith

quotes.text: A wise and frugal Government, which shall restrain men from injuring one another, shall leave them otherwise free to regulate their own pursuits of industry and improvement.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: I predict future happiness for Americans if they can prevent the government from wasting the labors of the people under the pretense of taking care of them.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: The democracy will cease to exist when you take away from those who are willing to work and give to those who would not.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: To compel a man to subsidize with his taxes the propagation of ideas which he disbelieves and abhors is sinful and tyrannical.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: A government big enough to give you everything you want is a government big enough to take from you everything you have.
quotes.author: Gerald Ford

quotes.text: We contend that for a nation to try to tax itself into prosperity is like a man standing in a bucket and trying to lift himself up by the handle.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: A nation of sheep will beget a government of wolves.
quotes.author: Edward R. Murrow

quotes.text: The more corrupt the state, the more numerous the laws.
quotes.author: Tacitus

quotes.text: Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely.
quotes.author: Lord Acton

quotes.text: The whole gospel of Karl Marx can be summed up in a single sentence: Hate the man who is better off than you are.
quotes.author: Henry Hazlitt

quotes.text: The real problem of humanity is that we have Paleolithic emotions, medieval institutions, and godlike technology.
quotes.author: E.O. Wilson

quotes.text: I prefer dangerous freedom over peaceful slavery.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: That government is best which governs least.
quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau

quotes.text: The only thing necessary for the triumph of evil is for good men to do nothing.
quotes.author: Edmund Burke

quotes.text: Experience should teach us to be most on our guard to protect liberty when the government's purposes are beneficent.
quotes.author: Louis Brandeis

quotes.text: Timid men prefer the calm of despotism to the tempestuous sea of liberty.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: Government spending is taxation. When you spend more, you're either going to have to borrow from future taxpayers, or you're going to have to increase taxation now.
quotes.author: Arthur Laffer

quotes.text: The only proper purpose of a government is to protect man's rights, which means: to protect him from physical violence.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: When you see that trading is done, not by consent, but by compulsion — when you see that in order to produce, you need to obtain permission from men who produce nothing — you may know that your society is doomed.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Do not ever say that the desire to make money is evil. It is the desire to make money by means of force, by extortion, by fraud — that is evil.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: Throughout the centuries there were men who took first steps, down new roads, armed with nothing but their own vision.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: The hardest thing in the world to understand is the income tax.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: A man who chooses between drinking a glass of milk and a glass of a solution of potassium cyanide does not choose between two beverages; he chooses between life and death.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: Under capitalism, the common man enjoys amenities which in ages gone by were unknown and therefore inaccessible even to the richest people.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: The market economy needs no apologists and propagandists. It can apply to itself the words of Sir Christopher Wren's epitaph in St. Paul's: if you seek his monument, look around.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: No one can find a safe way out for himself if society is sweeping towards destruction.
quotes.author: Ludwig von Mises

quotes.text: A government that is big enough to give you all you want is big enough to take it all away.
quotes.author: Barry Goldwater

quotes.text: Fiat money is the money of force, not of production and trade.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: I sincerely believe that banking establishments are more dangerous than standing armies, and that the principle of spending money to be paid by posterity under the name of funding is but swindling futurity on a large scale.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: Every government interference in the economy consists of giving an unearned benefit, extorted by force, to some men at the expense of others.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: A creative man is motivated by the desire to achieve, not by the desire to beat others.
quotes.author: Ayn Rand

quotes.text: We have a system that increasingly taxes work and subsidizes non-work.
quotes.author: Milton Friedman

quotes.text: I am a friend of free trade. God has put different resources in different regions of the globe so as to bring us all together in commerce.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: Treat each other not as combatants but as partners in a mutual search for truth.
quotes.author: Frederic Bastiat

quotes.text: It is always from a minority acting in ways different from what the majority would prescribe that new knowledge emerges.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The system of private property is the most important guaranty of freedom.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: Where there is no property there is no injustice.
quotes.author: John Locke

quotes.text: Government's first duty is to protect the people, not run their lives.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: Government is not the solution to our problem. Government is the problem.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: The nine most terrifying words in the English language are: I'm from the government and I'm here to help.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: Man is not free unless government is limited.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams.
quotes.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

quotes.text: To learn who rules over you, simply find out who you are not allowed to criticize.
quotes.author: Voltaire

quotes.text: He who sacrifices freedom for security deserves neither.
quotes.author: Benjamin Franklin

quotes.text: No government voluntarily reduces itself in size. Government programs, once launched, never disappear.
quotes.author: Ronald Reagan

quotes.text: Money won't create success, the freedom to make it will.
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: The inherent vice of capitalism is the unequal sharing of blessings. The inherent virtue of socialism is the equal sharing of miseries.
quotes.author: Winston Churchill

quotes.text: We shall reach the point where it will become far more economical to mine gold from sea water than to dig it from the earth. Then gold money, as we know it, will cease to exist.
quotes.author: Thomas Edison

quotes.text: The love of money, as distinguished from the love of money-getting, is the root of all evil.
quotes.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

quotes.text: I can calculate the motion of heavenly bodies, but not the madness of people.
quotes.author: Isaac Newton

quotes.text: In economics, the majority is always wrong.
quotes.author: John Kenneth Galbraith

quotes.text: Modern banking could not have arisen without states to back them.
quotes.author: Murray Rothbard

quotes.text: You have to choose between trusting the natural stability of gold and the honesty and intelligence of the members of the government. With due respect for these gentlemen, I advise you, for as long as the capitalist system lasts, to vote for gold.
quotes.author: George Bernard Shaw

quotes.text: Never let a good crisis go to waste.
quotes.author: Rahm Emanuel

quotes.text: The way to make money is to buy when blood is running in the streets.
quotes.author: John D. Rockefeller

quotes.text: The state does nothing well. It turns everything it touches to crap.
quotes.author: Andrew Napolitano

quotes.text: The urge to save humanity is almost always a false front for the urge to rule.
quotes.author: H.L. Mencken

quotes.text: Every election is a sort of advance auction sale of stolen goods.
quotes.author: H.L. Mencken

quotes.text: The whole aim of practical politics is to keep the populace alarmed — and hence clamorous to be led to safety — by menacing it with an endless series of hobgoblins, most of them imaginary.
quotes.author: H.L. Mencken

quotes.text: For every complex problem there is an answer that is clear, simple, and wrong.
quotes.author: H.L. Mencken

quotes.text: Giving money and power to government is like giving whiskey and car keys to teenage boys.
quotes.author: P.J. O'Rourke

quotes.text: The mystery of government is not how Washington works but how to make it stop.
quotes.author: P.J. O'Rourke

quotes.text: If you think health care is expensive now, wait until you see what it costs when it's free.
quotes.author: P.J. O'Rourke

quotes.text: When buying and selling are controlled by legislation, the first things to be bought and sold are legislators.
quotes.author: P.J. O'Rourke

quotes.text: The single biggest threat to man's continued dominance on the planet is the probability of civilization's collapse due to the actions of its own government.
quotes.author: Thomas Jefferson

quotes.text: I heartily accept the motto — that government is best which governs least. Carried out, it finally amounts to this — that government is best which governs not at all.
quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau

quotes.text: Trade and commerce, if they were not made of India rubber, would never manage to bounce over the obstacles which legislators are continually putting in their way.
quotes.author: Henry David Thoreau

quotes.text: Political language is designed to make lies sound truthful and murder respectable, and to give an appearance of solidity to pure wind.
quotes.author: George Orwell

quotes.text: In a time of universal deceit, telling the truth is a revolutionary act.
quotes.author: George Orwell

quotes.text: Economic control is not merely control of a sector of human life which can be separated from the rest; it is the control of the means for all our ends.
quotes.author: F.A. Hayek

quotes.text: The more laws, the less justice.
quotes.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

quotes.text: Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it.
quotes.author: George Bernard Shaw

quotes.text: The biggest risk is not taking any risk. In a world that's changing really quickly, the only strategy that is guaranteed to fail is not taking risks.
quotes.author: Mark Zuckerberg

quotes.text: The blockchain does for trust what the internet did for communication.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Not your keys, not your coins.
quotes.author: Andreas Antonopoulos

quotes.text: Bitcoin is the currency of resistance.
quotes.author: Max Keiser

quotes.text: Bitcoin changes absolutely everything.
quotes.author: Jack Dorsey

quotes.text: Stack sats.
quotes.author: Matt Odell

quotes.text: Fix the money, fix the world.
quotes.author: Bitcoin Proverb

quotes.text: Stay humble and stack sats.
quotes.author: Bitcoin Proverb

quotes.text: In the crypto world, everything that can be decentralized will be decentralized.
quotes.author: Naval Ravikant

quotes.text: Earn with your mind, not your time.
quotes.author: Naval Ravikant

quotes.text: The internet enabled free exchange of information; Bitcoin enables free exchange of value.
quotes.author: Naval Ravikant

quotes.text: Bitcoin is a tool for freeing humanity from oligarchs and tyrants, dressed up as a get-rich-quick scheme.
quotes.author: Naval Ravikant

quotes.text: We cannot solve our problems with the same thinking we used when we created them.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: Knowledge makes a man unfit to be a slave.
quotes.author: Frederick Douglass

quotes.text: Eternal vigilance is the price of liberty.
quotes.author: Wendell Phillips
